Empires & Puzzles Dunnar Hart Review: The Magmatic Drummer Strategy Guide

Dunnar Hart isn't just a bard; he's a one-orc heavy metal concert. With a 500% hit-all special and a drum solo that speeds him up to supersonic levels, he's here to burn the arena down. But can he survive long enough to drop the beat?
Dunnar Hart: The Heavy Metal Bard Who Melts Faces
Dunnar Hart, The Magmatic Drummer: Burning Beats and Broken Defenses
Dunnar Hart is what happens when you cross a high-fantasy bard with a death metal drummer and set the whole thing on fire. Hailing from the

event, this 5-star Legendary Fire hero is designed to end fights in a blaze of glory. Unlike his more delicate musical colleagues who strum lutes and gently heal wounds, Dunnar smashes drums filled with molten lava. He is a
mana hero who transitions into a speed demon once he gets his first special off, making him a terrifying engine of destruction if left unchecked.

Dunnar Hart's design is unapologetically "Heavy Metal." He features a muscular red demon-orc physique, flaming drumsticks, and a drum kit that looks like it was forged in the depths of a volcano. It's a loud, aggressive visual style that screams power.
Interestingly, the artwork sparked a bit of a "Satanic Panic" in the community due to the symbology on his bass drum, which some players felt resembled a pentagram. Whether you see it as a nod to the genre or something more sinister, there's no denying he has one of the most distinct and aggressive looks in the game. He definitely didn't come to play "Kumbaya."
, Dunnar Hart sits comfortably in the upper echelon of the game's stats, though he is slightly behind the absolute cutting edge of heroes released in late 2025 (who are pushing 1260+).

He is surprisingly bulky. This is critical because, as a Slow hero, he needs to take a beating before he can dish one out.
When fully limit broken, his stats allow him to survive the initial onslaught of even the most modern fast snipers.
"Infernal Drumstorm" is a special skill that effectively says, "If I fire, you lose."
is an absurd amount of raw damage. Even without defense down, this clears minions and severely softens up the entire enemy team.

Upon release, Dunnar Hart was immediately identified as a "Rush War God." His combination of heavy hitting and mana acceleration made him a staple in Very Fast formats.

While stats continue to climb (with late 2025 heroes hitting 1260+ power), Dunnar's 500% scaling and unique mechanics keep him relevant. He hasn't been "crept" out of viability because few heroes can match his total damage output and self-sustain engine.

Avoid pairing him with other Bard heroes unless you really need their specific skills, as you lose the passive mana bonus for solo heroes.

If you pulled Dunnar Hart early, congratulations! He is a "carry" hero. Even at slow speed, he will decimate lower-level teams and map stages single-handedly. Focus on keeping him alive with healers and defense buffs.

Dunnar is a specialized tool. He isn't the "slot him in everywhere" hero that some Fast heroes are. However, for Rush wars and specific defense formations, he is top-tier. He is worth the Limit Break materials if you care about Rush competitiveness or need a punishing red tank.
The community was split on his art. While many loved the "refreshingly new" rockstar vibe, others took issue with the "satanic" imagery on his drum kit.
Players immediately identified him as a successor to Khufu or Colen in Rush modes. One player noted,
"This card should just read: Goes off once you’re in big trouble... goes off again your whole team is dead."
There's a strong debate comparing him to
. While Ceris offers Silence (control), Dunnar offers pure, unadulterated damage.

Dunnar Hart is a hero that demands respect. He asks a simple question: "Can you kill me in 12 tiles?" If the answer is no, he will drum a rhythm of destruction that usually ends in a "Defeat" screen for the opponent. While his slow speed keeps him from being the undisputed king of the entire game, he is a virtuoso in his niche.
A rockstar for Rush Attack and a formidable tank. Crank the volume to 11 and let him play.
